title: $DO_NOT_USE_REGISTRY
text: $DO_NOT_USE_REGISTRY Specify whether the UHTTP component uses registry settings on Microsoft Windows systems. $DO_NOT_USE_REGISTRY { = } 0 | 1 Defaults Assignment file: application files Section: [PROXY_SETTINGS] Values 0 —use Registry settings if $SERVER and $BYPASS are omitted 1 —do not use Registry settings Default value: none Description By default, on Microsoft Windows, the UHTTP component uses the registry settings to determine the proxy server and the servers that can be accessed directly, bypassing the proxy server. You can override the registry settings using the $SERVER and $BYPASS assignment settings. You can use $DO_NOT_USE_REGISTRY to determine whether the UHTTP component uses registry settings on Microsoft Windows systems if $SERVER and $BYPASS are omitted. Example: $DO_NOT_USE_REGISTRY [PROXY_SETTINGS] $DO_NOT_USE_REGISTRY = 1 Copy Related Links UHTTP The Uniface UHTTP component is an HTTP client that is used for sending and receiving HTTP requests and responses. It provides operations for executing HTTP requests used for web applications and RESTful web services. $BYPASS Set the host identifiers or IP addresses to which the UHTTP component should connect directly,and not through a proxy server. $SERVER Set the proxy server to be used by the UHTTP component.
